summary: Added a dedicated assembly split for the MetroTRK exception vector table in .init, mapping 0x80003298-0x800051CC to src/MetroTRK/__exception.s. The PR represented this region in assembly rather than C because it is layout-sensitive and mixes embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions in one contiguous block. It also updated the GALE01 split file and taught the Nix configure step to pass devkitPPC binutils so dtk can assemble the new source without network downloads.
what_changed: Created src/MetroTRK/__exception.s as a new 156-line assembly file for the MetroTRK exception vector table.
Added a GALE01 split entry for MetroTRK/__exception.s covering .init start:0x80003298 end:0x800051CC with comment:0.
Moved the following .init range boundary so MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c now begins at 0x800051CC after the new exception-vector split.
Defined gTRKInterruptVectorTable and gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd labels around the contiguous vector-table block.
Encoded the table contents with .asciz, .balign, .skip padding, branch instructions, and exception-vector comments for offsets such as 0x0100, 0x0200, 0x0300, 0x0F00, 0x1000, and 0x1F00.
Introduced assembly macros trk_redirect and trk_tlb_redirect to avoid repeatedly spelling out the PowerPC redirect prologue and TLB-specific CR/MSR handling.
Updated .nix/melee.nix to pass --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin to configure.py, allowing dtk to use sandbox-available binutils instead of attempting an on-the-fly download.
smart_moves: Chose assembly for a layout-sensitive .init exception-vector region instead of forcing a C representation that would be brittle or misleading.
Used .skip counts to preserve fixed-size gaps between exception vectors and keep the contiguous table layout aligned to hardware vector offsets.
Factored repeated exception redirect code into trk_redirect and trk_tlb_redirect macros, making a large assembly table more auditable while preserving exact emitted code patterns.
Named global bounds as gTRKInterruptVectorTable and gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd, making the split searchable and giving future code/linker references stable symbols.
Handled the Nix failure by wiring the existing devkitPPC binutils path into configure.py, preserving reproducible non-networked builds rather than relying on dtk downloading binaries.
Referenced a similar OOT GC decomp MetroTRK __exception.s implementation as precedent, which is useful for cross-project decomp patterns.
decomp_lessons: For exception vectors and other hardware tables, exact layout may matter more than high-level source representation; assembly can be the right decomp artifact when the region mixes data, padding, labels, and instructions.
When adding assembly sources that require external assembler support, check sandboxed/reproducible build environments early; a normal build may silently download tools that Nix will correctly reject.
A new split can expose latent tooling dependencies. Document or configure those dependencies explicitly rather than relying on implicit dtk behavior.
Macros are acceptable and useful in hand-written matching assembly when they reduce repeated boilerplate without hiding layout-critical padding or labels.
Split boundaries should be updated carefully around .init regions so the next source file starts exactly at the new end address.

Cross-referencing equivalent decomp projects can guide representation of vendor/runtime components like MetroTRK, but the PR evidence only directly cites OOT GC as a reference, not as proof of identical code.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK exception assembly file named src/MetroTRK/__exception.s, matching the double-underscore style used for low-level runtime/exception components.
Global table symbols use g-prefixed CamelCase names: gTRKInterruptVectorTable and gTRKInterruptVectorTableEnd.
Assembly macros use lowercase snake_case descriptive names: trk_redirect and trk_tlb_redirect.
Local labels use .L_ prefix, with macro-unique suffixing via \@ for .L_restore_cr_\@.
Exception-vector comments include explicit hexadecimal vector offsets and human-readable exception names, e.g. '# 0x0300: DSI Exception'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used .section .init, "ax" to place the vector table in the executable init section.
Used .balign 4 before the table and after the initial .asciz string to maintain 4-byte alignment.
Used .asciz "Metrowerks Target Resident Kernel for PowerPC" followed by .skip padding to represent embedded string data inside the vector region.
Used .skip with exact byte counts to land subsequent snippets at expected exception vector offsets.
Used PowerPC special-purpose registers SPRG1, SPRG2, SPRG3, SRR0, and SRR1 in the redirect macro to save registers/state and redirect to TRKInterruptHandler.
Used rfi after setting SRR0/SRR1 to transfer control to TRKInterruptHandler with the vector number loaded in r3.
Used a specialized trk_tlb_redirect path for PPC603E TLB miss vectors that saves/restores CR and manipulates MSR with sync/mtmsr before falling through to trk_redirect.
Represented the 0x0F00 Performance Monitor / 0x0F20 AltiVec Unavailable overlap with a branch to .L_perf_monitor, a 28-byte skip, and separate redirect sequences.
Preserved reset handling as a direct branch to __TRK_reset at vector 0x0100.
Passed --binutils ${devkitppc}/bin in Nix so the assembler required for this .s file is available to dtk under sandboxed builds.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Nix failed because the PR added a binutils/assembler dependency; regular builds download dtk binaries on the fly, but Nix disallows network access for reproducibility.
review_feedback.outcome: The Nix expression was updated to pass the devkitPPC binutils directory to configure.py.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether adding all of binutils was worthwhile for an unused MetroTRK component, but noted the sandbox likely already had binutils and configure just needed to be told to use it.
review_feedback.outcome: The dependency was accepted after discussion and fixed with a simple Nix configure flag.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether dtk required all of binutils or only the assembler, and suggested using gc-wii-binutils if needed.
review_feedback.outcome: r-burns replied that only the assembler appeared necessary and that upstream binutils were already packaged for Nix.

follow_up_queries: Find other layout-sensitive .init or exception-vector regions that should be represented as assembly rather than C.
Check whether all build environments pass an explicit binutils path when dtk needs to assemble .s files.
Search for MetroTRK split boundaries around 0x80003298 and 0x800051CC to confirm later PRs did not regress this layout.
Look for additional MetroTRK low-level runtime components where OOT GC decomp has comparable assembly references.
Audit whether comments on exception vector offsets remain accurate if any .skip counts are edited.
